messages.content: In a wxwidgets c++ project, I have a wxArrayString with list of different categories, and I have another wxArrayString which contains elements of each category. I want to create a new wxArrayString with the category and its elements count, and this format of "[count] category", how can I achieve this?

it is giving me an error in the line wxString categoryCountString = wxString::Format(“[%d] %s”, count, category);,
the error is: 
/usr/local/include/wx-3.1/wx/strvararg.h(480): assert "(argtype & (wxFormatStringSpecifier<T>::value)) == argtype" failed in wxArgNormalizer(): format specifier doesn't match argument type

Backtrace:
ASSERT INFO:
/usr/local/include/wx-3.1/wx/strvararg.h(480): assert "(argtype & (wxFormatStringSpecifier<T>::value)) == argtype" failed in wxArgNormalizer(): format specifier doesn't match argument type

BACKTRACE:
[1] wxArgNormalizer<unsigned long>::wxArgNormalizer(unsigned long, wxFormatString const*, unsigned int) /usr/local/include/wx-3.1/wx/strvararg.h:480
[2] wxArgNormalizerWchar<unsigned long>::wxArgNormalizerWchar(unsigned long, wxFormatString const*, unsigned int) /usr/local/include/wx-3.1/wx/strvararg.h:500
[3] wxString wxString::Format<unsigned long, wxString>(wxFormatString const&, unsigned long, wxString) /usr/local/include/wx-3.1/wx/string.h:2329
using u is giving me the same error
that is giving me the same error

now the last way worked perfectly, now it works perfect but I want to do add a new functionality, I want to sort the categories based on its elements count, so the higher count goes first. How can I add this functionality?
I don't want to use an struct, can you make it without using it?

error: no matching function for call to ‘stoi(wxString)’|

it is giving me this error at the lines: size_t countA = std::stoi(a.AfterFirst('[').BeforeFirst(']'));

I manage to solve the problem like this:
size_t countA = std::stoi(a.AfterFirst('[').BeforeFirst(']').ToStdString());

and it works!

But now I have another issue, since the elements are all correct now I got something like this:

[4] D
[2] F
[0] Z
[0] X
[0] Y
...

And I want the 0 values to have the original order:

[4] D
[2] F
[0] A
[0] B
[0] C
...
it is giving me this error: 'categories' is not captured
it is giving me this error: no matching function for call to ‘wxArrayString::Sort(Utils::GetCategoriesCount(const wxArrayString&, const wxArrayString&)::<lambda(const wxString&, const wxString&)>)’|
